title: Linked List Cycle Detection
pattern: Linked List â€” Fast and Slow Pointers (Floyd's Cycle Detection)
coreIdea: Detect if a linked list has a cycle by moving two pointers at different speeds; if a cycle exists, the faster pointer will eventually catch up to the slower one, otherwise it reaches the end.
recognitionCues: asks to detect a cycle in a linked list
O(1) space constraint typically specified, ruling out a visited-set approach
approach: Initialize slow and fast pointers at head. Move slow by one step and fast by two steps each iteration. If they ever meet, a cycle exists. If fast reaches null (end of list), no cycle exists.
